rkagi 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • rkagi Quickstart Guide

Create a new Kagi summarize request

new_kagi_summarize.Rd

Construct a typed S3 object of class kagi_summarize that describes a Universal Summarizer request. Use kagi_perform() to execute the request and obtain a new_kagi_summarize_results() object.

Usage

new_kagi_summarize(
  conn,
  url = NULL,
  text = NULL,
  engine = c("cecil", "agnes", "muriel", "daphne"),
  summary_type = c("summary", "takeaway"),
  target_language = c("EN", "BG", "CS", "DA", "DE", "EL", "ES", "ET", "FI", "FR", "HU",
    "ID", "IT", "JA", "KO", "LT", "LV", "NB", "NL", "PL", "PT", "RO", "RU", "SK", "SL",
    "SV", "TR", "UK", "ZH", "ZH-HANT"),
  cache = TRUE
)

Arguments

conn

A kagi_connection object created by new_kagi_connection().

url

Optional character scalar. URL to be summarized. Mutually exclusive with text.

text

Optional character scalar. Raw text to be summarized. Mutually exclusive with url.

engine

Character scalar. Summarizer engine, one of "cecil", "agnes", "muriel", "daphne".

summary_type

Character scalar. Type of summary requested, one of "summary" or "takeaway".

target_language

Character scalar. Target language (ISO code such as "EN", "FR", "DE", …).

cache

Logical. Whether to allow API-side caching.

Value

A kagi_summarize object to be passed to kagi_perform().

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
conn <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "summarize")
req <- new_kagi_summarize(conn, text = "Lorem ipsum")
req
} # }
